sources.notes: Same hard-version Beautiful Patterns task as 2174C2 under the Div.2 entry.
Official tutorial link for the shared round.
Interval-pair classification and aggregation for the hard version.

annotation.summary: Compute E[number_of_palindromic_substrings^2] for a random string, optimized for the hard constraints.
annotation.constraints: Direct pair enumeration is not acceptable; pair contributions need grouping by interval relation.
annotation.core_idea: Expand the square into ordered pairs of palindrome indicators. Count equality components for each pair type, then group overlapping/nested/disjoint interval pairs so their probabilities can be summed by prefix aggregates instead of per-pair DSU.
annotation.complexity: Near-linear or low-polynomial after grouping interval classes.

solution_variants.name: grouped-second-moment
solution_variants.summary: Group palindrome-event pairs by interval geometry and sum probabilities.

tags.evidence: The expected square is evaluated by summing ordered pairs of indicator events.
Each contribution counts color assignments under equality constraints.
The hard version uses grouped cumulative sums over interval parameters.
The main optimization distinguishes disjoint, nested, and crossing substring pairs.
